question: Broad knowledge of human genetics & molecular biology
Broad knowledge of genetic and genomic analytical and testing methodologies
Following protocols, standard operating procedures (SOPs) and guidelines
Tolerance / acceptance of uncertainty and ambiguity
Maintaining good records and documentation of dry lab work / clinical work (e.g., spreadsheets, reports)
Presenting to / teaching non-scientists or non-medical professionals
Effectively and succinctly synthesizing / summarizing complex information to generate insights
Training and mentoring individuals
Seeking advice and asking for feedback / clarification from advisors and mentors when needed
Demonstrating workplace etiquette (e.g., punctuality, consideration, flexibility, gratitude)
Complying with rules, guidelines, policies, laws, and regulations
Upholding commitments and meeting deadlines
Working effectively in a hierarchical team (e.g., collaboration, sharing timely updates, willingness to take on tasks for the good of the project / team, reporting to a team lead / supervisor)
Adapting to changing goals, projects, and circumstances at work
Working under pressure / managing tight deadlines
Building and maintaining positive relationships with colleagues, and an encouraging, supportive, and psychologically safe workplace culture
Providing constructive feedback
Demonstrating responsible writing practices (i.e., referencing appropriately)
Demonstrating accountability for one’s actions, and a dedication to acting in accordance with the highest standards of professionalism
Recruiting and tracking patients in clinical trials, including patient consenting and data collection
Attending conferences or scientific meetings
Analyzing and managing financial data or budgets
